Add emoji-free mode for terminals without emoji fonts (#315) (#339)

Infrastructure:
- EMOJI_ASCII map (55 entries) in status_constants.py with ASCII fallbacks
  for all emoji used in the TUI
- emoji_or_ascii() helper and ctx.e() method on ColumnContext
- emoji_free field on ColumnContext, SessionSummary, and TUIPreferences
- Setting persisted in ~/.overcode/preferences.json

Toggle:
- 'E' keybinding toggles emoji-free mode
- action_toggle_emoji_free() in tui_actions/view.py
- Help overlay updated with new keybinding

Render functions updated:
- Status indicators (🟢→[R], 🔴→[W], etc.)
- Permission modes (🔥→B!, 🏃→P>, 👮→N:)
- Tool icons (🖥️→Sh, ✏️→Wr, etc.)
- Activity indicators (🔔, 🤿, 🐚, 👶, 📋, 🤝, etc.)

- Human/robot counts, time context, standing orders

commit.message:
feat: context-aware first-message response with date/time/quantity detection

- TS layer now responds directly when first message has date context:
  "E depois das 18 amanhã 2 horário" → "Boa noite! Para amanhã a partir das 18h (2 horários), qual serviço você quer? 😊"
- Add quantity extraction: "2 horário(s)", "3 vagas", "2 pessoas" stored as requestedQuantity
- Fix bug: old date-in-first-message block had !session.data.date guard which was always false after date pre-extraction; new block uses session.data.date (already extracted)
- Block skips when specific service keyword present (corte, barba, cabelo...) — AI handles those
- Includes prof name + time hint + quantity in the contextual reply
- Prompt: explicit "NÃO cumprimente" instruction when first message has context (effectiveShouldGreet=false)
- requestedQuantity added to CONTEXTO ATUAL so AI knows client wants N slots

commit.message:
Fix vats_start.bat trailing backslash quote escaping issue

The %~dp0 variable includes a trailing backslash which caused
pip install -e "%SCRIPT_DIR%" to fail with:
  ERROR: C:\tools\vats" is not a valid editable requirement

The trailing backslash escaped the closing quote, causing pip to
interpret the path as having a literal quote character.

Fix: Strip the trailing backslash using %SCRIPT_DIR:~0,-1%
substring operation after initial assignment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: call _dispose() on _rintf services when their allowed caller disconnects (#152)

* fix: call _dispose() on _rintf services when their allowed caller disconnects

When _cleanupRintfForCaller removes _rintf services on caller disconnect,
call _dispose() on each service before deleting it. This ensures the RPC
system's cleanup path is properly invoked and application code can eagerly
react to caller disconnection without waiting for the next _rintf_timeout.

Fixes #151

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(python): call _dispose() on _rintf services when their allowed caller disconnects

Mirror the JavaScript fix to the Python RPC implementation:
_cleanup_rintf_for_caller now calls _dispose() (sync or async) on each
_rintf service before removing it, giving application code an eager
cleanup signal when the allowed caller disconnects.

Also adds a Python integration test that verifies _dispose() is called
when the allowed caller (provider) disconnects from the workspace.

Fix auto-archive timing, announcement pinning, and date display

  computing dates from week numbers (week numbers are scoring periods,
  not calendar weeks - Heisman is week 22 but happens before the NC game)

  instead of relative "Today" / "Yesterday" / "Xd ago"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reduce tilt sensitivity: Tilt Curve 1.4→2.0, Tilt Smooth 0.25→0.4 (fixes #81)

Higher response curve (2.0) makes small tilts produce proportionally less
lean input (squared falloff), reducing drastic reactions to slight quick
movements. Higher output smoothing (0.4) filters sharp spikes from jerky
inputs while preserving full-range responsiveness.

commit.message:
fix: handle pnpm symlinks in node_modules scanner (#41)

* docs: use npx @tanstack/intent for all CLI command references

Users rarely install global packages, so show the npx invocation everywhere.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: handle pnpm symlinks in node_modules scanner

Dirent.isDirectory() returns false for symlinks, causing scanForIntents
to skip every package in pnpm-managed node_modules. Accept symlinks too
since they always point to directories in node_modules.

Fixes #39

Add .env.example template

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: plugin.json repository must be a string (#21)

## Summary
- Fix `repository` field in `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` from npm-style
object to plain string URL

## Root cause
Claude Code plugin manifest validation expects `repository` to be a
string, not an object:
```
✘ Failed to install plugin "pathfinder": Plugin has an invalid manifest file.
Validation errors: repository: Invalid input: expected string, received object
```

## Change
```diff
-  "repository": {
-    "type": "git",
-    "url": "https://github.com/srpadrono/Pathfinder.git"
-  },
+  "repository": "https://github.com/srpadrono/Pathfinder",
```

## Test plan
- [ ] `claude plugin install pathfinder` succeeds without validation
errors

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Fix streaming: model IDs + race condition

1. Fix invalid model IDs:
   - VPS: claude-haiku-4 → claude-haiku-4-5-20251001
   - OpenRouter: anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5 → anthropic/claude-3-5-haiku-20241022
   Lead finder was failing 100% on OpenRouter with "not a valid model ID"

2. Fix streaming race condition:
   - Don't start background analysis on page load
   - Let SSE endpoint start it when client connects
   - This ensures SSE connection is open BEFORE data starts flowing
   - Previously: analysis finished before SSE connected → static page

3. Better SSE init: wait up to 2s for progress dict initialization

commit.message:
Add simulation report and expand derivations with full step-by-step algebra

Results folder (new):
- results/simulation_report.md: comprehensive 12-section report covering
  convergence diagnostics, factor recovery, survival performance (C-index
  0.827 PCA vs 0.828 Supervised), GEP top-feature tables, and roadmap
  for real multi-omics applications
- results/figures/: 8 figure pairs (PDF + PNG) — RMSE trace, ELBO proxy,
  beta comparison, GEP heatmap, Kaplan-Meier, signal recovery, loading
  correlations, tau distribution
- results/*.csv: all numeric summary tables (11 files)
- results/run_simulation.R: standalone script to reproduce all outputs
- results/simulation_console_output.txt: verbatim simulation log

Derivations (expanded):
- MF_Derivations_UpdateAlgo_REVISED.tex: rewritten from ~650 → 1199 lines
  with full intermediate algebra throughout every update section:
  • Sec 3 (Taylor): 15-step completing-the-square derivation with working
    response z_i = u_i/W_ii + eta_hat_i
  • Sec 4 (L update): full residual substitution, squared-term expansion,
    E_q[.] application, A/B identification for genomics + survival
  • Sec 5 (F update): explicit absence of F in survival, full quadratic
    grouping to get A_{jk}, B_{jk} with corrected j subscript (R4)
  • Sec 6 (beta update): g(beta) log-expectation derivation, variance-mean
    decomposition, cross-term expansion, mean-field independence argument,
    error-in-variables A_k = sum_i W_ii E[l^2_ik] (R5, R6)
  • Sec 7 (tau update): full R^2_ij expansion under mean-field, explicit
    d/dtau optimisation step, constrained forms (R7, R8)
  New derivbox tcolorbox (gray) for algebra steps, distinct from
  correctionbox (red) and ebnmbox (blue)
- MF_Derivations_UpdateAlgo_REVISED.pdf: recompiled (21 pages, was 12)
- MF_V2_Companion.pdf: recompiled (17 pages, consistent)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
